enonce.texte:
Read the following instructions about solving common phone problems carefully:

HOW TO SOLVE COMMON PHONE PROBLEMS

Problem 1: Phone battery drains too quickly
• Reduce screen brightness
• Turn off Wi-Fi and Bluetooth when not using them
• Close apps that you are not using
• Check which apps use the most battery

Problem 2: Phone freezes or is very slow
• Restart your phone
• Delete unnecessary files and apps
• Clear app cache
• Update your phone's operating system

Problem 3: Phone storage is full
• Delete old photos and videos you don't need
• Move files to cloud storage or an external memory card
• Uninstall apps you never use

Problem 4: Wi-Fi connection problems
• Turn Wi-Fi off and on again
• Forget the network and reconnect
• Restart your router
• Check if other devices can connect

questions.texte: List three things you can do to save battery life on your phone.
questions.reponse: Three things you can do to save battery life are: 1) Reduce screen brightness, 2) Turn off Wi-Fi and Bluetooth when not using them, and 3) Close apps that you are not using.

questions.texte: What should you do if your phone freezes or is very slow?
questions.reponse: If your phone freezes or is very slow, you should restart your phone, delete unnecessary files and apps, clear app cache, and update your phone's operating system.

questions.texte: Compare the solutions for a slow phone and a phone with full storage.
questions.reponse: Both problems require deleting unnecessary files. For a slow phone, you also need to restart, clear cache, and update the system. For full storage, you specifically need to delete old photos and videos, move files to cloud storage, and uninstall unused apps.

intitule: Exercise 2: Identifying Gadget Parts and Symbols

enonce.texte:
Match each symbol or part with its correct name and function.

List of symbols/parts:

  • 🔋 Battery icon
  • 📶 Wi-Fi symbol
  • 📱 Screen
  • 🔌 Charging port
  • 🎧 Headphone jack
  • 📷 Camera

questions.texte: What does the battery icon show you?
questions.reponse: The battery icon shows you how much battery power is left on your device.

questions.texte: What is the function of the Wi-Fi symbol?
questions.reponse: The Wi-Fi symbol shows that your device is connected to a wireless internet network.

questions.texte: Explain the purpose of the charging port on a phone.
questions.reponse: The charging port is where you plug in the charger to recharge your phone's battery. It is also used to connect your phone to a computer for data transfer.

intitule: Exercise 3: Giving Instructions - Solving Gadget Problems

enonce.texte: Write step-by-step instructions on how to solve the following gadget problems. Use sequencing words (first, next, then, finally).

questions.texte:
Problem: Your phone screen is cracked.
Instructions: Write 5 steps on what to do.
questions.reponse:
Instructions:

  1. First, turn off your phone to avoid electrical shock.
  2. Next, apply a screen protector to prevent glass from falling out.
  3. Then, back up all your data to a cloud service or computer.
  4. After that, take your phone to a professional repair shop.
  5. Finally, ask the technician to replace the screen with a new one.

questions.texte:
Problem: Your phone keeps overheating.
Instructions: Write 5 steps on what to do.
questions.reponse:
Instructions:

  1. First, remove your phone case to help it cool down.
  2. Next, close all apps that are running in the background.
  3. Then, turn off features like GPS, Bluetooth, and Wi-Fi.
  4. After that, place your phone in a cool, shaded area away from direct sunlight.
  5. Finally, if the problem continues, restart your phone or contact the manufacturer.

intitule: Exercise 4: Writing Tips for Gadget Care

enonce.texte:
Write a brochure or tips sheet (120-150 words) on how to take care of your phone or tablet.

Your tips should cover:

  • Battery care
  • Screen protection
  • Storage management
  • Security tips
  • Regular maintenance

questions.texte: Write a tips sheet for taking care of your phone or tablet.
questions.reponse:
Example Tips Sheet:

HOW TO TAKE CARE OF YOUR PHONE

🔋 Battery Care: Charge your phone when the battery drops below 20%. Avoid charging overnight. Use the original charger.

🛡️ Screen Protection: Apply a screen protector and use a phone case. Keep your phone away from keys and coins that can scratch the screen.

💾 Storage Management: Delete unused apps and old photos regularly. Use cloud storage for important files.

🔒 Security Tips: Use a password, PIN, or fingerprint lock. Download apps only from official stores.

🧹 Regular Maintenance: Clean your phone with a soft cloth. Restart your phone weekly to refresh the system.

enonce.texte:
Look at these common smartphone symbols and answer the questions:

SymbolNameFunction
📶Signal barsShows cellular network strength
🔊SpeakerControls sound output
🌐InternetIndicates internet connection
🔒LockIndicates security/encryption

questions.texte: What do signal bars indicate on your phone?
questions.reponse: Signal bars show the strength of your cellular network connection. More bars mean a stronger signal.

questions.texte: Explain the difference between the speaker symbol (🔊) and the internet symbol (🌐).
questions.reponse: The speaker symbol (🔊) controls sound output - you can use it to increase or decrease volume. The internet symbol (🌐) indicates whether your phone has an internet connection and shows its strength.

questions.texte: When you see the lock symbol (🔒) on your phone, what does it mean?
questions.reponse: The lock symbol (🔒) indicates that your connection is secure and encrypted, meaning your data is protected from unauthorized access.

enonce.texte:
Write a dialogue (10-12 exchanges) between a customer and a technician about a phone problem.

Your dialogue should include:

  • Greeting and introduction of the problem
  • Description of the symptoms
  • Technical questions from the technician
  • Solutions or advice
  • A conclusion

questions.texte: Write a dialogue about solving a phone problem.
questions.reponse:
Example Dialogue:

Customer: "Good morning. My phone keeps freezing and restarting by itself. Can you help me?"

Technician: "Good morning. Let me see. How long has this been happening?"

Customer: "For about a week now. It started after I downloaded a new app."

Technician: "Have you tried restarting your phone?"

Customer: "Yes, I have. But the problem continues."

Technician: "Let me check your storage. You have very little storage space. That could be the problem."

Customer: "What should I do?"

Technician: "First, delete the app you downloaded. Then, clear your cache and delete old photos. If the problem continues, you may need to reset your phone to factory settings."

Customer: "Will I lose all my data?"

Technician: "Yes, so please back up your data first. I can help you with that."

Customer: "Thank you very much. I appreciate your help."

Technician: "You're welcome. Come back if you need further assistance."

enonce.texte:
Read the following words related to gadgets and place the stress on the correct syllable. Write the stressed syllable in capital letters.

Example: computer → comPUter

questions.texte: battery
questions.reponse: BATtery

questions.texte: iPhone
questions.reponse: IPhone (or eye-PHONE in some contexts, but usually the first syllable is stressed)

questions.texte: Android
questions.reponse: ANdroid

questions.texte: Bluetooth
questions.reponse: BLUEtooth

questions.texte: application
questions.reponse: ap - pli - CA - tion

questions.texte: technology
questions.reponse: tech - NOL - o - gy

questions.texte: internet
questions.reponse: INter - net

questions.texte: computer
questions.reponse: com - PU - ter

intitule: Exercise 8: Writing Instructions for a Gadget Problem

enonce.texte:
Write a clear set of instructions (150-180 words) to help someone who has lost all data on their phone.

Your instructions should include:

  • What to do immediately
  • How to recover data (if possible)
  • How to prevent this problem in the future
  • Use sequencing words (first, then, next, finally)

questions.texte: Write instructions for someone who has lost data on their phone.
questions.reponse:
Example Instructions:

HOW TO RECOVER LOST PHONE DATA

First, stay calm and do not panic. Do not use your phone too much as this might overwrite the lost data.

Next, check if you have a backup. Look in Google Photos, iCloud, or other cloud services. If you have a backup, restore your data from there.

Then, if you don't have a backup, try using data recovery software. Connect your phone to a computer and run a recovery program. This can find deleted files.

After that, if the data is very important, take your phone to a professional data recovery service. They have special tools to recover lost data.

Finally, to prevent this problem in the future, always back up your data regularly. You can set automatic backups to cloud services. Also, be careful when deleting files and avoid clicking on suspicious links.

enonce.texte:
Label the parts of a smartphone using the words in the box.

Words: screen, camera, charging port, volume button, home button, speaker, headphone jack, power button

[Diagram of a smartphone with blank labels]
images: A simple diagram of a smartphone showing the following parts: a large screen in the middle, a camera at the top front, a home button at the bottom front, volume buttons on the left side, a power button on the right side, a charging port at the bottom, a headphone jack at the bottom, and a speaker at the bottom.

questions.texte: What is the large part in the middle of the phone called?
questions.reponse: The large part in the middle of the phone is called the screen.

questions.texte: Where do you plug in your charger?
questions.reponse: You plug in your charger at the charging port, which is usually located at the bottom of the phone.

questions.texte: Which buttons are usually on the side of the phone?
questions.reponse: The volume buttons and the power button are usually on the side of the phone.

enonce.texte:
Write a short story (200-250 words) about someone who faced a serious problem with their phone or tablet and how they solved it.

Your story should include:

  • A clear setting and characters
  • A description of the gadget problem
  • The steps taken to solve the problem
  • The resolution and what was learned
  • Use at least 5 vocabulary words from the lesson

questions.texte: Write a story about a gadget problem and how it was solved.
questions.reponse:
Example Story:

"The Day My Phone Stopped Working"

It was the morning of my final exams. I woke up and reached for my phone to check the time. But the screen was blank. I pressed the power button, but nothing happened. My phone was completely dead!

I panicked. All my study notes, schedule, and important contacts were on that phone. I tried to plug in the charger, but the charging port seemed damaged. I didn't know what to do.

First, I asked my mother if I could use her phone. Then, I called a technician who came to our house. He examined my phone and said that the problem was with the battery. It had swollen and needed to be replaced.

The technician replaced the battery and also fixed the charging port. He told me that I should not let my phone overheat and that I should use the original charger. He also advised me to back up my data regularly.

After two hours, my phone was working again. I was so relieved! I managed to save my study notes and passed my exams. From that day on, I always back up my data and take better care of my phone. I learned a valuable lesson: never take your gadgets for granted!
